questions.text: What is the primary purpose of revealing the Quran according to the opening verses?
questions.answer: As a reminder for those who fear Allah, not to cause the Prophet distress.

questions.text: How does Allah describe His knowledge and dominion early in the Surah?
questions.answer: He knows the secret and what is even more hidden; to Him belongs all that is in the heavens and the earth.

questions.text: Where did Musa (AS) first perceive the fire and hear the voice of Allah?
questions.answer: In the direction of Mount Tur, in the sacred valley of Tuwa.

questions.text: What was Musa (AS) commanded to do when he approached the fire?
questions.answer: To remove his sandals, for he was in the sacred valley of Tuwa.

questions.text: What is the very first thing Allah told Musa (AS) about Himself?
questions.answer: 'Indeed, I am Allah. There is no deity except Me, so worship Me and establish prayer for My remembrance.'

questions.text: What is the warning regarding the Hour (Day of Judgment) mentioned to Musa?
questions.answer: That it is coming, and Allah has kept it hidden so that every soul may be recompensed for what it strives for.

questions.text: What were the two miracles given to Musa (AS) at the start of his mission?
questions.answer: His staff turning into a moving serpent and his hand shining white without disease.

questions.text: What was the specific prayer Musa (AS) made to Allah before facing Pharaoh?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, expand for me my breast, ease for me my task, and untie the knot from my tongue that they may understand my speech.'

questions.text: Why did Musa (AS) ask for his brother Harun to accompany him?
questions.answer: To be his helper and to share in his task, because Harun was more eloquent in speech.

questions.text: What favor did Allah remind Musa (AS) of regarding his infancy?
questions.answer: How his mother was inspired to place him in a chest and cast him into the river.

questions.text: Who was watching Musa (AS) from a distance as he floated in the river?
questions.answer: His sister.

questions.text: How did Musa (AS) return to his mother after being picked up by Pharaoh's family?
questions.answer: His sister suggested their mother as a wet-nurse, and Allah thus returned him to her so she would not grieve.

questions.text: What was the incident in Egypt that caused Musa (AS) to flee to Madyan?
questions.answer: He killed a man (an Egyptian) and then fled from the city out of fear.

questions.text: What was the instruction to Musa and Harun regarding how to speak to Pharaoh?
questions.answer: 'Speak to him with gentle speech that perhaps he may be reminded or fear [Allah].'

questions.text: What was Pharaoh's question to Musa regarding 'previous generations'?
questions.answer: He asked, 'Then what is the condition of the former generations?'

questions.text: What was Musa's response to Pharaoh's question about previous generations?
questions.answer: He said the knowledge of them is with my Lord in a Record; my Lord neither errs nor forgets.

questions.text: What signs of nature are mentioned as proof of Allah's power in this Surah?
questions.answer: The earth as a bed, the paths made through it, rain from the sky, and various kinds of vegetation.

questions.text: What was Pharaoh's reaction to the nine signs shown by Musa (AS)?
questions.answer: He denied them all and accused Musa of being a magician who wanted to drive them out of their land.

questions.text: What day was chosen for the public contest between Musa and the magicians?
questions.answer: The day of the festival (Yawm al-Zinah), at mid-morning.

questions.text: What was the magicians' initial attitude before the contest?
questions.answer: They were confident and asked Pharaoh if they would have a reward if they were the victors.

questions.text: What happened when the magicians threw their ropes and staffs?
questions.answer: They appeared to Musa, by their magic, to be moving serpents, which caused Musa to feel a slight fear.

questions.text: What did Allah tell Musa when he felt fear during the contest?
questions.answer: 'Fear not. Indeed, it is you who are superior. Throw what is in your right hand; it will swallow up what they have crafted.'

questions.text: What was the reaction of the magicians when Musa's staff swallowed their illusions?
questions.answer: They fell down in prostration, declaring, 'We believe in the Lord of Harun and Musa.'

questions.text: What was Pharaoh's threat to the magicians after they believed?
questions.answer: To cut off their hands and feet on opposite sides and crucify them on the trunks of palm trees.

questions.text: What was the magicians' courageous response to Pharaoh's threat?
questions.answer: They said, 'No harm! Indeed, to our Lord we will return. We hope that our Lord will forgive us our sins because we were the first of the believers.'

questions.text: What did Allah command Musa to do when the time came to leave Egypt?
questions.answer: To travel by night with His servants and strike for them a dry path through the sea.

questions.text: What happened to Pharaoh and his army in the sea?
questions.answer: The sea overwhelmed them completely and they were drowned.

questions.text: What were the 'good things' Allah provided for the Children of Israel in the desert?
questions.answer: Manna and quails (Salwa).

questions.text: Why did Musa (AS) hasten to the mountain ahead of his people?
questions.answer: He said, 'They are close upon my tracks, and I hastened to You, my Lord, that You might be pleased.'

questions.text: What trial did the people face in Musa's absence at the mountain?
questions.answer: They were misled by Samiri into worshipping a golden calf.

questions.text: How did Samiri justify the creation of the calf?
questions.answer: He claimed he saw something they didn't see (the tracks of the messenger/angel) and took a handful of dust from it to throw into the melting jewelry.

questions.text: How did Harun (AS) try to stop the people from worshipping the calf?
questions.answer: He told them they were only being tested by it and reminded them that Allah is Ar-Rahman, but they refused to listen until Musa returned.

questions.text: What did Musa (AS) do to his brother Harun in his initial anger upon returning?
questions.answer: He seized him by his head (hair) and beard, demanding to know why he hadn't followed him.

questions.text: What was Harun's defense to Musa?
questions.answer: He said, 'O son of my mother, do not seize me by my beard or by my head. I feared that you would say, "You caused division among the Children of Israel."'

questions.text: What was the punishment for Samiri?
questions.answer: He was told that in his life he would have to say, 'Touch me not,' being isolated from people, and he would have a punishment in the Hereafter.

questions.text: What did Musa (AS) do with the golden calf?
questions.answer: He burned it and then scattered its dust thoroughly into the sea.

questions.text: Who is the only true God according to the end of the story of Musa?
questions.answer: 'Your god is only Allah, except whom there is no deity. He has encompassed all things in knowledge.'

questions.text: What happens to the one who turns away from the 'Reminder' (the Quran)?
questions.answer: He will bear a heavy burden on the Day of Resurrection and will abide in it forever.

questions.text: What will happen to the mountains on the Day of Judgment according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Allah will blast them into dust, leaving the earth a level plain with no curves or hills.

questions.text: Will intercession benefit anyone on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: No, except for him to whom the Most Merciful has given permission and has accepted his word.

questions.text: Why was the Quran revealed in the Arabic language?
questions.answer: So that people may fear Allah or that it may cause them to remember.

questions.text: What prayer is the Prophet (SAW) taught regarding his knowledge of the Quran?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, increase me in knowledge' (Rabbi zidni 'ilma).

questions.text: What was the 'covenant' Allah took from Adam before?
questions.answer: A command not to eat from the tree, but Adam forgot and did not have a firm resolve.

questions.text: What did Shaitan whisper to Adam to tempt him?
questions.answer: He said, 'O Adam, shall I direct you to the Tree of Eternity and a kingdom that will not deteriorate?'

questions.text: What happened immediately after Adam and Hawa ate from the tree?
questions.answer: Their private parts became apparent to them, and they began to fasten over themselves the leaves of Paradise.

questions.text: How did Allah deal with Adam after his mistake?
questions.answer: His Lord chose him and turned to him in forgiveness and guided him.

questions.text: What is the consequence for those who turn away from the Quran as mentioned in a specific ayah?
questions.answer: They will have a 'depressed life' (Ma'ishatan Dhanka) and be raised blind on the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What will the blind person ask his Lord on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: 'My Lord, why have you raised me blind while I was [once] seeing?'

questions.text: What will be Allah's response to the blind person?
questions.answer: 'Thus did Our signs come to you, and you forgot them; and thus will you this Day be forgotten.'

questions.text: Is the punishment of the Hereafter more severe or more enduring?
questions.answer: It is both more severe and more enduring.

questions.text: What is the instruction to the Prophet regarding the timing of prayer?
questions.answer: To exalt Allah before the rising of the sun and before its setting, and during the hours of the night and at the ends of the day.

questions.text: Should the Prophet be envious of the worldly pleasures given to some disbelievers?
questions.answer: No, they are only a flower of the life of this world by which Allah tests them; the provision of your Lord is better and more enduring.

questions.text: Is the Prophet commanded to force his family to pray?
questions.answer: He is commanded to enjoin prayer on his family and be steadfast in it himself.

questions.text: Does Allah ask for provision from us?
questions.answer: No, He says, 'We do not ask you for provision; We provide for you, and the [best] outcome is for [those of] righteousness.'

questions.text: What is the 'clear proof' that the disbelievers demanded, which already existed?
questions.answer: The previous scriptures (like the Torah and Gospel) which contain the same core message.

questions.text: What is the final command to the Prophet regarding the wait for judgment?
questions.answer: To say: 'Each [of us] is waiting; so wait. For you will know who are the companions of the sound path and who is guided.'

questions.text: What is the meaning of the title 'Ta-Ha'?
questions.answer: These are 'Muqatta'at' (disconnected letters) whose exact meaning is known only to Allah, though they serve as a name for the Prophet in this context.

questions.text: What is the 'Beautiful Names' (Al-Asma al-Husna) mentioned early in the Surah?
questions.answer: They belong to Allah, the one God, except whom there is no deity.

questions.text: What is the 'sacred valley' named in the story of Musa?
questions.answer: Tuwa.

questions.text: What is the 'white hand' miracle a sign of?
questions.answer: One of the great signs of Allah to establish Musa's prophethood.

questions.text: What was the 'knot' on Musa's tongue?
questions.answer: A speech impediment he had, which he asked Allah to heal for his mission.

questions.text: What is the 'minister' (Wazir) Musa asked for?
questions.answer: A helper from his family, specifically his brother Harun.

questions.text: What was the 'River' (Yam) where baby Musa was cast?
questions.answer: The Nile River.

questions.text: What is the 'eye' of Allah mentioned in the context of Musa's upbringing?
questions.answer: It refers to Allah's continuous protection and care over him.

questions.text: What was the 'fear' of Musa and Harun about facing Pharaoh?
questions.answer: They feared he would hasten against them or transgress.

questions.text: What is the 'Lord of the worlds' definition according to Musa in this Surah?
questions.answer: The One who gave everything its form and then guided it.

questions.text: What is the 'magical illusion' mentioned in the contest?
questions.answer: The tricks the magicians used to make their ropes seem like they were moving.

questions.text: What is the 'superiority' Allah promised Musa?
questions.answer: That his truth would overcome their falsehood.

questions.text: What is the 'magic' of the magicians called?
questions.answer: The plot of a magician, which never succeeds wherever he comes.

questions.text: What is the 'eternal reward' the magicians hoped for?
questions.answer: The forgiveness of Allah and Paradise.

questions.text: What is the 'dry path' in the sea?
questions.answer: The miraculous way made for the Children of Israel to escape.

questions.text: What is the 'trial' of the calf?
questions.answer: A test of faith for the Children of Israel after being saved from Pharaoh.

questions.text: What is the 'dust' from the messenger's tracks?
questions.answer: A mysterious element Samiri claimed to use to give life to the calf.

questions.text: What is the 'lowering' of the voice on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: Out of fear and respect for the Most Merciful.

questions.text: What is the 'hasting' with the Quran?
questions.answer: The Prophet's attempt to recite it before its revelation was complete.

questions.text: What is the 'Tree of Eternity'?
questions.answer: The false name Shaitan gave to the forbidden tree to tempt Adam.

questions.text: What is the 'descent' to earth?
questions.answer: The expulsion of Adam, Hawa, and Iblis from Paradise.

questions.text: What is the 'depressed life' (Ma'ishatan Dhanka)?
questions.answer: A life without peace, purpose, or spiritual satisfaction.

questions.text: What is the 'flower' of worldly life?
questions.answer: The temporary and fragile beauty of wealth and power.

questions.text: What is the 'sound path' (Sirat-is-Sawi)?
questions.answer: The straight and correct way of life guided by Allah.
